SWIFTSELL is a web-based Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ) platform that helps your sales team build accurate quotes faster, manage customer relationships, and process orders — all in one place.
App Configuration Types define the schema for App Configurations — they specify what a configuration value represents and how it should be used by the application.
App Configurations store platform-level settings that control application behavior. Each configuration belongs to a type and holds a value that the application reads at runtime.
The Addresses section contains a master list of all addresses in the system. Addresses are used for billing and shipping on quotes and orders.
The Customers section manages the customer accounts that can be associated with quotes and orders. Customers can be created manually here or may be created automatically when a quote is submitted.
SWIFTSELL integrates with Infor CloudSuite (CSI) to synchronize orders, pricing, and product configuration data with your ERP system.
Feature Flags enable or disable optional functionality. Flags are scoped to a platform and can be created, updated, or deleted by Super Admins. Changes take effect immediately.
The Imports section allows administrators to bulk-import data into SwiftSell from files. This is useful for initial data setup or ongoing synchronization of large data sets.
The Issue Reports section collects error and feedback reports submitted by users from within the application.
License Management is where the platform owner manages the SWIFTSELL subscription and license details.
The Administration section is the control panel for your SWIFTSELL platform. Access it from the main navigation bar — it is only visible to users with System Admin role level or higher.

SWIFTSELL supports multi-tenant deployments through Platforms. Each platform is a distinct instance with its own users, products, customers, configuration, and branding. The Platforms list is a Super Admin feature; System Admins manage their own platform's settings via Platform Settings.
The Products section is where administrators manage the product catalog — the items that users can configure and add to quotes.
Product Groups organize products into categories for filtering and display purposes in the catalog.
Product Lists control which products are visible to specific groups of users and can be used to apply group-specific pricing.
The All Quotes view gives System Admins a platform-wide view of every quote created by any user.
The RMAs section lets administrators create and manage return requests. An RMA can be initiated from an order or created directly from this page.
Status Types define the stages that quotes and orders can be assigned to. Each status type has a name, a display order, a minimum role level required to assign it, and a visibility flag.
SMTP Settings configure the outbound email server SWIFTSELL uses to send system emails. This section is built on top of the App Configuration system using the PLATFORM_EMAIL configuration type.
Themes control the visual appearance of SWIFTSELL for a given platform. Each platform can have multiple themes defined — one active light theme and one active dark theme at a time.

Groups organize users into segments that share a restricted customer and a product list. When a user belongs to a group that has a Default Customer set, they can only see and select that one customer on quotes — all other customers are hidden. When the group has an Assigned Product List, only the products on that list appear in the catalog for members of the group.
Role Management lets Super Admins create and configure the named roles available on the platform. Roles are assigned to users and determine their access level throughout SWIFTSELL.
The Users section lets administrators view and manage all user accounts on the platform.
Accessing SWIFTSELL
Main Navigation
SWIFTSELL uses role-based access control to determine what each user can see and do. Your role is assigned by an administrator.
The Configurator walks you through selecting options for a product step by step. When you finish, the configured product is added as a line item on your quote.
The Orders page shows all orders you have placed — quotes that have been submitted and converted to orders.
The Product Catalog is the starting point for building a quote. It lists all products available on your platform and can be browsed without logging in.
The Attachments section appears at the bottom of the Lines tab on a quote. Use it to upload supporting documents such as drawings, specifications, or customer-provided files.
When a quote is ready to submit as an order, use the Summary tab to review and check out.
There are two ways to start a new quote in SWIFTSELL.

A quote in SWIFTSELL is a collection of configured product line items with pricing, customer details, and supporting information. Quotes are used to present pricing to a customer and ultimately convert to an order.
The Lines tab is the main working area of a quote. Each line represents one configured product.
SWIFTSELL is a web-based Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ) platform that helps your sales team build accurate quotes faster, manage customer relationships, and process orders — all in one place.

Browse the product catalog — search and filter available products, view pricing and specifications. Configure products — use the guided configurator to select options and build a product to your customer's exact needs. Build and manage quotes — add configured products as line items, apply pricing, attach supporting documents, and communicate with your team. Submit orders — check out directly from a quote and track the order through fulfillment. Manage returns — submit and track Return Merchandise Authorizations (RMAs).
documents.s: What can you do with SWIFTSELL?

documents.t: Section Audience User Guide Sales reps and anyone who creates quotes or places orders Admin Guide Platform administrators who configure and manage SWIFTSELL Not sure where to start? Head to Logging In.

App Configuration Types define the schema for App Configurations — they specify what a configuration value represents and how it should be used by the application. note Managing configuration types requires Super Admin access.
Go to Administration → App Configuration Types. TODO Add screenshot of the App Configuration Types list
Click Create Configuration Type. Enter a name and description. Define the platform(s) this type applies to. Click Save.
Click any type row to open and edit it.

Configuration Types act as templates. Once a type is defined, one or more App Configurations can be created using that type to provide runtime values. TODO Document all existing configuration types and their purpose
App Configurations store platform-level settings that control application behavior. Each configuration belongs to a type and holds a value that the application reads at runtime.
Go to Administration → App Configurations. (Requires Super Admin or higher.) TODO Add screenshot of the App Configurations list
Configurations are listed with their name, type, platform assignment, and current value. Use the search and filter controls to find a specific configuration.
Click Create Configuration. Select a Configuration Type (defines the schema and purpose of the value). Assign it to a platform if applicable. Enter the value. Click Save.
Click any configuration row to open the edit form. Update the value and click Save.

Use the delete action on the configuration to remove it. Ensure the application does not depend on the configuration before deleting. TODO Document the specific configuration types and their effects on the application
App Configuration Types
The Addresses section contains a master list of all addresses in the system. Addresses are used for billing and shipping on quotes and orders.
Go to Administration → Addresses. TODO Add screenshot of the Addresses list
Addresses are listed sorted alphabetically. Each record includes street, city, state/province, postal code, and country.
Click Create Address. Fill in the address fields. Click Save.
Click any address row to open and edit it. TODO Confirm whether addresses are managed standalone here or are always tied to a customer record

The Customers section manages the customer accounts that can be associated with quotes and orders. Customers can be created manually here or may be created automatically when a quote is submitted. info Only customers with an ERP ID set are available in the customer auto-fill dropdown on quote pages.
Go to Administration → Customers. TODO Add screenshot of the Customers list

Each row shows the customer's ERP ID, company name, billing address, and platform. Use the search and sort controls to find a specific customer. Click the edit (pencil) icon to open the customer form. Click the delete (trash) icon to delete the customer immediately (no confirmation prompt — use with care).
Click Create Customer. Fill in the customer form:

Field Notes Assign to Platform Required. The platform this customer belongs to. Name The company name ERP ID The customer's ID in your ERP system. Required for the customer to appear in quote auto-fill. Description Optional, up to 500 characters
Field Notes First Name Primary contact first name Last Name Primary contact last name Email Primary contact email Phone Primary contact phone number

Field Notes Address Line 1 Max 50 characters Address Line 2 Max 50 characters City Max 30 characters State US state select Zip Max 16 characters Shipping Address is same as Billing? When checked, no separate shipping addresses can be added and billing is used for shipping on quotes Click Save. After saving you can add shipping addresses (if billing and shipping are not the same).

If Shipping Address is same as Billing? is unchecked, a Assign Shipping Addresses table appears below the form after the customer is saved. From here you can: Click Create Address to add a new shipping address via a modal form. Click the edit icon on any address to modify it. Click the delete icon to remove it. These shipping addresses become available in the shipping address dropdown on the Customer tab of any quote for this customer.
Click the edit icon on any customer row to open the customer form.
Click the delete icon on any customer row. The deletion is immediate.

When a user logs in for the first time without an existing customer record, the system automatically creates a Customer (along with a Contact and a default Address) for that user. These auto-created customers will appear in this list. They typically have no ERP ID and will not appear in the quote customer auto-fill dropdown until an ERP ID is assigned.

A customer record has a Group field (groupId) that links it back to a group. This is set when a group's Default Customer is configured — that customer is the one restricted to users in that group. If a group has a Default Customer assigned, users in that group will see only that customer in the customer selection on quotes. To change which customer is restricted to a group, update the Default Customer field on the group record — see Groups.
Addresses Groups — groups restrict customer access via their Default Customer setting
SWIFTSELL integrates with Infor CloudSuite (CSI) to synchronize orders, pricing, and product configuration data with your ERP system.
Go to Administration → ERP Integration. TODO Add screenshot of the ERP Integration configuration page

When configured, the ERP integration enables: Sending submitted orders from SWIFTSELL to the ERP system Receiving order confirmation and ERP order numbers back into SWIFTSELL Using the Infor CloudSuite configurator engine for product configuration rules and pricing
TODO Document the full ERP integration configuration fields — connection URL, credentials, tenant settings, etc.
Products can be configured to use either a local configurator or the Infor CloudSuite (Cloud) configurator. This is set per-product in the Products administration page via the Server field.
TODO Document common ERP integration errors and how to resolve them
Feature Flags enable or disable optional functionality. Flags are scoped to a platform and can be created, updated, or deleted by Super Admins. Changes take effect immediately.
Go to Administration → Feature Flags. (Requires Super Admin or higher.) TODO Add screenshot of the Feature Flags page
Flags are listed with their name and current state. Each flag is associated with a platform.
Click Create (from the page header) to add a new flag. Provide a name and assign it to a platform. TODO Document the full fields on the create flag form
Each flag item has controls to update its value. Changes invalidate the cached flag state for all users on the platform and take effect on their next interaction.

Each flag item has a delete action. Deleting a flag disables the feature it controls for all users. caution Deleting a feature flag that is actively in use will disable that functionality for all users immediately. Verify the impact before deleting.

The following flags are referenced in the application code: Flag Name Effect When Enabled productGroups Shows Product Groups in the catalog filter and adds Product Groups to the Administration dashboard traceFiles Adds Trace Files to the Administration dashboard rma Enables the RMA workflow — users can create returns from orders; RMAs appear in Administration demoAccounts Enables the demo login route and demo signup page quoteRequests Shows the Quote Requests table on the Quote Dashboard TODO Confirm the full current list of flags and exact names as they appear in the database
The Imports section allows administrators to bulk-import data into SwiftSell from files. This is useful for initial data setup or ongoing synchronization of large data sets.
Go to Administration → Imports. TODO Add screenshot of the Imports dashboard

The Imports dashboard shows four import categories: Type Description Products Import product records into the catalog Customers Import customer records Shipping Addresses Import shipping address records Users Import user accounts Click any tile to open that import page.
TODO Document the import file format, required columns, and step-by-step process for each import type
TODO Document whether import results/logs are shown and how errors are handled
The Issue Reports section collects error and feedback reports submitted by users from within the application. note Issue Reports require Super Admin access.
Go to Administration → Issue Reports. TODO Add screenshot of the Issue Reports list
Reports are listed sorted by most recently submitted. Each row shows the reporter, submission date, and a summary of the issue.

Click any row to open the full issue report, which includes: The user who submitted it A description of the issue Any relevant context captured at submission time TODO Document whether screenshots or session data are captured with reports
TODO Document whether reports have a status/resolution workflow or if they are informational only
License Management is where the platform owner manages the SWIFTSELL subscription and license details. note License Management requires Owner Super Admin access.
Go to Administration → License Management. TODO Add screenshot of the License Management page
TODO Document the license management fields and actions — subscription tier, seat count, expiration, etc.
TODO Document the process for updating or renewing a license
The Administration section is the control panel for your SWIFTSELL platform. Access it from the main navigation bar — it is only visible to users with System Admin role level or higher.
Click Administration in the main navigation bar. You will see a card grid of all sections available to your role level. TODO Add screenshot of the Administration card grid

Not all cards are visible to every admin. What you see depends on your role level and enabled feature flags. Section Minimum Role Feature Flag Required Users System Admin — Groups System Admin — Products System Admin — Product Lists System Admin — Customers System Admin — Themes System Admin — RMAs System Admin — ERP Integration System Admin — Product Groups System Admin productGroups Status Types System Admin — Platform Settings System Admin — All Orders System Admin — All Quotes System Admin — Role Management Super Admin — Platforms Super Admin — Issue Reports Super Admin — Feature Flags Super Admin — App Configuration Types Super Admin — App Configurations Super Admin — SMTP Settings Super Admin — Trace Files Super Admin traceFiles License Management Owner Super Admin — Create Sales Demo Owner Super Admin —
Users & Roles Platforms Products App Configuration Themes

SWIFTSELL supports multi-tenant deployments through Platforms. Each platform is a distinct instance with its own users, products, customers, configuration, and branding. The Platforms list is a Super Admin feature; System Admins manage their own platform's settings via Platform Settings.
Go to Administration → Platforms. (Requires Super Admin or higher.) TODO Add screenshot of the Platforms list
Each row shows the Platform ID, Name, and the email addresses configured to receive order notifications. Click any row to open that platform's settings form.
Click Create Platform. Fill in the Platform Settings form (see fields below). Click Save.

Platform Settings are accessible to System Admins via Administration → Platform Settings, and to Super Admins by clicking any platform in the Platforms list. Field Description Platform Name The display name for this platform Order Confirmation Notifications One or more email addresses that receive a notification whenever a user places an order. Use + / − to add or remove addresses. Integration Type The ERP integration to use: Visual, CSI, SWIFTSELL, or none (default) CSI Order Complete Identifier (CSI only) The status label in CSI that indicates a completed order (e.g., Ordered). Must match exactly what is configured in CSI. Require Multi-Factor Authentication for all users When enabled, all users on this platform must use MFA — the option cannot be turned off per-user Require HTTPS Forces the configurator to use HTTPS when making requests to configuration servers

Most admin sections (Users, Groups, Customers, Themes, RMAs, Product Lists) are scoped to a platform. When you navigate to those sections, you are always viewing or managing data for your current platform. Super Admins can switch between platforms by updating the Platform field on their own user profile.
The Products section is where administrators manage the product catalog — the items that users can configure and add to quotes.
Go to Administration → Products. TODO Add screenshot of the Products list
Products are listed sorted by product number. Each row shows the product number, name, and status.

Click Create Product. Fill in the product details: Product number Name and description Pricing information Configurator settings (server, header ID, etc.) Image Click Save. TODO Document all fields on the product form in detail
Click any product row to open the product form. Make your changes and click Save.

Some products use the Infor CloudSuite configurator engine (Cloud) and some use the local configurator. The Server field on the product determines which engine is used. Products can also be marked as Quick Config — these appear as one-click shortcuts on the Quote Dashboard. TODO Document the isQuickConfig and isNonConfigurable flags and their effects
Product Groups Product Lists

Product Groups organize products into categories for filtering and display purposes in the catalog. note Product Groups must be enabled via the Feature Flags section before they appear in Administration.
Go to Administration → Product Groups. (Requires the productGroups feature flag to be enabled.) TODO Add screenshot of the Product Groups list
Click Create Product Group. Enter a name for the group. Assign products to the group. Click Save.
Click any group row to open and edit it. You can rename the group or change its product assignments.
TODO Confirm delete behavior — does it unassign products or block deletion if products are assigned?
Product Groups appear as filter options in the Product Catalog, allowing users to narrow the product list by category.
documents.s: How Product Groups Affect the Catalog

documents.t: Product Lists control which products are visible to specific groups of users and can be used to apply group-specific pricing.
Go to Administration → Product Lists. TODO Add screenshot of the Product Lists list

Click Create Product List. Enter a name for the list. Assign products to the list. Associate the list with a group or platform. Click Save. TODO Document all fields and the relationship between Product Lists, Groups, and pricing
Click any list row to open and edit it.
TODO Explain how product lists restrict/override the default catalog for assigned groups
The All Quotes view gives System Admins a platform-wide view of every quote created by any user.
Go to Administration → All Quotes. TODO Add a screenshot of All Quotes list

Quotes are listed sorted by most recently created. Each row shows: Column Description Quote # The SWIFTSELL quote ID Created By The user who created the quote Customer The customer associated with the quote Status Current quote status Total Quote total Created Date Date the quote was created
Click any row to open the quote. Admins can view and edit quotes belonging to any user on the platform.
Use the search bar and filters to find quotes by status, date range, user, or customer.

The RMAs section lets administrators create and manage return requests. An RMA can be initiated from an order or created directly from this page. note The RMA feature must be enabled via Feature Flags before it is available.
Go to Administration → RMAs. TODO Add screenshot of the RMAs list
RMAs are listed sorted by most recently created. Each row shows the RMA ID, the linked quote/order, and the ERP order number.
RMAs can be created two ways: From an order — open an order and click Create RMA (visible when the RMA feature flag is enabled). From this page — click Create RMA and fill in the form manually.
An RMA consists of a header record and one or more detail lines. Each detail line represents a product being returned.

Click Add Line to create a new detail line. Each line has: Field Description Product Number The part number of the item being returned Model Number The model number of the item Quantity The quantity being returned Reason A description of why the item is being returned Click the delete icon on any line to remove it. Lines cannot be deleted after the RMA has been submitted.

Once all detail lines are filled in, click Submit RMA. The system validates that each line with a quantity has a product number, model number, and reason set before submitting. warning Once an RMA is submitted, it is locked — detail lines can no longer be added, edited, or deleted. The submitted state is tracked by the rmaCreated flag on the RMA record.
Status Types define the stages that quotes and orders can be assigned to. Each status type has a name, a display order, a minimum role level required to assign it, and a visibility flag.
Go to Administration → Status Types. (Requires System Admin or higher.) TODO Add screenshot of the Status Types list
Status types are displayed sorted by Display Order (ascending). Each entry shows the name and current settings. Click any status type to open the edit form. Click Create Status Type to add a new one.

The form opens as a modal overlay with these fields: Field Description Name The label shown to users (e.g., "Pending Approval", "Approved", "Shipped") Display Order A number that controls the sort order in lists. Lower numbers appear first. Minimum Role Level The minimum role required to assign this status to a quote or order. Select from the available roles via the radio button cards. Is Visible When unchecked, this status type is hidden from users — useful for internal-only statuses or retired stages.
documents.s: Creating or Editing a Status Type

documents.t: Open the status type and click Delete. caution Deleting a status type that is currently assigned to quotes or orders may leave those records with a stale or missing status. Verify the status is not in use before deleting.

SMTP Settings configure the outbound email server SWIFTSELL uses to send system emails. This section is built on top of the App Configuration system using the PLATFORM_EMAIL configuration type. note SMTP Settings require Super Admin access. Changes may take up to 5 minutes to take effect.
Go to Administration → SMTP Settings. TODO Add screenshot of the SMTP Settings page

The SMTP Settings page includes a platform selector at the top. Select the platform you want to configure email settings for, then fill in the fields below. TODO Document the specific SMTP fields shown by the AppConfigurator for the PLATFORM_EMAIL type or by inspecting the configuration type definition
SWIFTSELL sends emails for: Password reset requests TODO Confirm all email triggers — order confirmations, quote notifications, user invites, etc.
App Configurations App Configuration Types
Themes control the visual appearance of SWIFTSELL for a given platform. Each platform can have multiple themes defined — one active light theme and one active dark theme at a time.
Go to Administration → Themes. TODO Add screenshot of the Themes list

Themes are displayed as cards. The currently active light and dark themes for your platform are marked with a sun (☀) or moon (🌙) tag respectively. From the list you can: Click a theme card to open and edit it Use the Copy action on a card to duplicate a theme as a starting point Use the Delete action to remove a theme
Click Create Theme. Fill in the theme form. Click Save.

Field Description Platform Required. The platform this theme applies to. Name A label for the theme (e.g., "Light Default", "Dark Corporate") Description Optional notes Dark Mode When enabled, this theme is treated as a dark theme Is Active Theme When enabled, this becomes the active theme for the platform (light or dark depending on the Dark Mode setting)
Field Description Logo The logo used in light mode Logo (Dark) The logo used in dark mode Email Logo The logo included in system emails

Field Description Primary Main brand color — used for primary buttons and highlights Secondary Accent color Header Color Background color of the top navigation bar Header Text Color Text color of the navigation bar Call to Action Color Color for call-to-action elements Background Color Page background Grid Row Background Color Row background in tables Border Color Color for borders and dividers Tertiary Third accent color Gray Light gray used for secondary elements Gray Dark Darker gray Box Shadow Color Color for drop shadows Text Dark Primary text color Danger Color for destructive actions and error states Success Color for success states and confirmations Warning Color for warning states
Field Description Border Radius Controls the roundness of UI elements Breakpoints Responsive breakpoint widths (array of pixel values)
To make a theme active for your platform, open it and check Is Active Theme, then save. This replaces the current active theme of the same type (light or dark).

Groups organize users into segments that share a restricted customer and a product list. When a user belongs to a group that has a Default Customer set, they can only see and select that one customer on quotes — all other customers are hidden. When the group has an Assigned Product List, only the products on that list appear in the catalog for members of the group.
Go to Administration → Groups. TODO Add screenshot of the Groups list

Click Create Group. Fill in the group form: Field Notes Assign to Platform Required. The platform this group belongs to. Name The group's display name Default Customer Optional. When set, users in this group can only see this customer — all other customers are hidden from them on quotes. Only customers with an ERP ID are available here. Description Optional free-text description Assign to Product List Optional. When set, only the products on this list are visible to users in this group. Click Save. After saving you are taken to the group detail page where you can assign users.

After creating a group (or opening an existing one), a Assigned Users table appears below the group form. This table lists all users on the platform and shows whether each user is currently in the group. Click the + (green) button on a user row to add them to the group. Click the − (red) button to remove them. The Assigned indicator column turns green for users currently in the group. note A user can belong to multiple groups. Their groupIds field stores all group memberships.
Click any group card from the Groups list to open the group form. Changes to the Default Customer or Product List take effect on the next quote created by users in that group.
Open the group and click Delete. This removes the group record but does not delete the users who were members.

Platform └── Group ├── Product List → restricts catalog to only these products for members ├── Default Customer → restricts quote customer selection to only this customer └── Users (many) → assigned via the group form A user can belong to multiple groups. Internally, each user record stores all of their group memberships as a list (groupIds) and tracks their currently active group (groupId). The group assignment is managed from the group form here — not from the user profile. info Customers also store a back-reference to their group (groupId). This means a customer record can be directly associated with a group, and that association is visible on the customer record. Product Lists are managed under Administration → Products → Product Lists. Customers are managed under Administration → Customers. Users are assigned a group via the group form here, not from the user form.
documents.s: How Groups Connect to the Rest of the System

documents.t: Role Management lets Super Admins create and configure the named roles available on the platform. Roles are assigned to users and determine their access level throughout SWIFTSELL.
Go to Administration → Role Management. (Requires Super Admin or higher.) TODO Add screenshot of the Roles list

A role has a name, a description, and a Role Access Level. The access level maps to one of the system's fixed permission tiers — it's the access level that actually controls what the user can do, not the name. Access Level Permission Tier 0 Public User 1 Registered User 5 System Admin 9 Super Admin note Owner Super Admin (level 20) cannot be created through the Role Management UI — it is a system-level designation.

Click Create Role. A modal form opens. Fill in: Role Name — the label users and admins will see (e.g., "Sales Rep", "Sales Manager") Description — a brief note about what this role is for Role Access Level — select the permission tier this role grants Click Save.
Click any role card to open the edit form in a modal. Update the fields and save.
danger Deleting a role that is assigned to active users will break the application for those users. Before deleting a role, ensure no users are currently assigned to it.
Roles are assigned on the User form. The User Role dropdown shows all roles whose access level is less than or equal to your own.
documents.s: Assigning a Role to a User

documents.t: The Users section lets administrators view and manage all user accounts on the platform.
Go to Administration → Users. TODO Add screenshot of the Users list

Users are displayed in a table sorted by most recent activity. Each row shows: Column Description Email The user's email address (also their username) Last Name The user's last name Online A status indicator — green if the user was active in the last 5 minutes Role Level The role assigned to the user Platform (Super Admin only) The platform the user belongs to info System Admins see only users on their own platform. Super Admins see all users across all platforms.

Click Create User. Fill in the user form: Field Notes Email Address Required. Used as the username. Must be a valid email. Cannot be changed after creation. First Name Optional Last Name Optional User Role Select from available roles. You can only assign roles up to your own role level. Checkout Type Place Order — submits directly to the ERP; Self Checkout (Stripe) — user pays via Stripe Platform (Super Admin only) The platform to assign this user to Password Required on create. Must contain 8+ characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character Confirm Password Must match Password Click Save. The user is created immediately and can log in with the credentials you set.
Click any user row to open their profile. All fields except Email Address can be updated. To change the password, click Update Password to reveal the password fields.
TODO Confirm the current delete flow
A user can enable Multi-Factor Authentication from their own profile page. If the platform has MFA required, the option is locked on and cannot be disabled by the user.

If a user has a demo account expiration date set, Super Admins will see a DEMO ACCOUNT panel at the top of their profile with: The demo source An expiration date picker To deactivate a demo account immediately, set the expiration date to a date in the past.

Each user is linked to a Customer record. If no customer exists for a user when they first log in, the system automatically creates one (along with a default Contact and Address). This auto-created customer will appear in the Customers list. If you want a user to be associated with a specific customer (for example, a real company account with an ERP ID), assign a group to them that has that customer set as its Default Customer. See Groups for how that restriction works.
Roles Groups Customers
Open SWIFTSELL in your web browser and navigate to your organization's SWIFTSELL URL.
On the login screen, enter your username and password. Click Log In. If your account requires two-factor authentication (2FA), you will be prompted to complete that step before continuing.

Click Forgot Password on the login screen. Enter your email address and click Send Reset Link. Check your email for a password reset link and follow the instructions. note Password reset emails are sent from your organization's configured SMTP address. If you don't see the email, check your spam folder or contact your administrator.

If your organization allows self-registration: Click Register on the login screen. Fill in your name, email, and a password. If 2FA enrollment is required, you will be guided through that setup immediately after registering.

SWIFTSELL supports 2FA for enhanced account security. If 2FA is enabled for your account: You will be prompted to enter a verification code after your password. Codes are typically generated by an authenticator app (such as Google Authenticator or Authy). If you need to set up or reset 2FA, contact your administrator.
Click your profile menu in the top navigation bar and select Log Out. You can also navigate directly to /auth/logout.

The top navigation bar is present on every page and provides access to the main areas of SWIFTSELL. TODO Add annotated screenshot of the navigation bar Area Description Home Your starting point after login Product Catalog Browse and search all available products Quotes View and manage your quotes Orders View your submitted orders Profile Manage your account settings Administration Admin-only section for managing the platform (visible to admins only)

After logging in you are taken to the Welcome screen. This provides quick access to common actions such as starting a new quote or browsing the catalog. TODO Describe the welcome page tiles/widgets in detail

Most list pages (product catalog, quotes, orders) include: A search bar to filter by name or keyword. Filter controls to narrow results by category, status, date, and other attributes. Sort options to change the order of results.
Pages that drill down (e.g., Quote → Line Item → Configurator) show breadcrumbs near the top of the page so you can navigate back to a parent view.
SWIFTSELL uses role-based access control to determine what each user can see and do. Your role is assigned by an administrator.

Role Description Public User Not logged in — can browse the catalog and access auth pages Registered User Logged-in standard user — can configure products, manage their own quotes and orders System Admin Full access to the Administration section Super Admin System Admin access plus advanced platform configuration Owner Super Admin Full access to everything, including license management
Home and Welcome pages Login, Register, Forgot Password, Password Reset Product Catalog
Everything a Public User can access, plus: Product Configurator Quote Dashboard (their own quotes) Individual quote pages Their own Orders Profile page

Everything a Registered User can access, plus the Administration section: Users management Groups Products, Product Lists Customers and Addresses Themes RMAs ERP (CSI) Integration Status Types Platform Settings All Quotes and All Orders (across the platform) Imports Product Groups (if enabled by feature flag)

Everything a System Admin can access, plus: Role Management Platforms (multi-tenant management) Issue Reports Feature Flags App Configuration Types App Configurations SMTP Settings Create Sales Demo Trace Files (if enabled by feature flag)
Everything a Super Admin can access, plus: License Management
Your current role is visible on your Profile page. If you need a different level of access, contact your SWIFTSELL administrator.
The Configurator walks you through selecting options for a product step by step. When you finish, the configured product is added as a line item on your quote.

The Configurator is divided into pages of options. Each page presents a set of choices — you work through them in order and move to the next page when all required selections are made. TODO Add screenshot of the configurator form with options

Area Description Top bar Shows the product name and progress through pages Options form The current page of configuration choices Product image Updates as you make selections to reflect your configuration (sticky on desktop) Bottom bar Navigation controls, current price, and the Finish button

Work through each option on the page. Required fields must be completed before advancing. The product image on the right updates to reflect your current choices. Informational messages may appear as you configure — read them carefully as some may indicate important constraints.
Use the Next and Back buttons in the bottom bar to move between pages. You can also jump to a completed page directly.

When all required options are selected, click Finish on the bottom bar. SWIFTSELL will: Finalize your configuration with the pricing engine. Add it as a new line item on your quote. Return you to the quote page.

If the product supports rapid-line entry, you will be asked: Would you like to start another configuration with this same product? Selecting Yes opens the configurator again. You can optionally choose to retain your previous selections as a starting point.
From the quote's Lines tab, click the edit icon on any line to re-open it in the Configurator with your previous selections loaded.

Click Cancel in the bottom bar to discard the current configuration and return to your quote without adding a line. note If you are not logged in, finishing a configuration creates a Quote Request instead of a standard quote. A sales representative will follow up with you.
The Orders page shows all orders you have placed — quotes that have been submitted and converted to orders.
Navigate to your profile menu and select My Orders, or click Orders in the navigation bar. TODO Add screenshot of the Orders page

Orders are displayed in a table sorted by most recently placed. Each row shows: Column Description Order # The ERP order number (assigned after submission) Quote # The original SWIFTSELL quote ID Date Date the order was placed Status Current order status Total Order total
Click any order row to open the order detail view. This is the same quote page but in read-only mode — lines, customer details, and attachments are visible but cannot be edited.
Order statuses are configured by your administrator. Common statuses include: TODO List the actual status types configured for this platform

If the RMA feature is enabled on your platform and you need to return items from an order, a Create RMA button will be available on the order page. See your administrator or contact support for the return process.
The Product Catalog is the starting point for building a quote. It lists all products available on your platform and can be browsed without logging in.
Navigate to the Product Catalog from the main navigation bar. Products are displayed in a grid or list view. TODO Add screenshot of the catalog grid
Use the search bar to find products by name or keyword. Use the filter panel to narrow results by product group or category. Toggle between grid view and list view using the view controls.
Each product card shows: Product name and image Base price A short description Click a product card to open the Product Preview, which shows full details before you start configuring.

From the catalog or product preview, click Configure to open the product in the Configurator. This will either add the product to an existing quote or prompt you to create a new one. Quick Configure If your administrator has set up Quick Configure products, they will appear at the top of your Quote Dashboard for fast one-click access.

The Attachments section appears at the bottom of the Lines tab on a quote. Use it to upload supporting documents such as drawings, specifications, or customer-provided files. TODO Add screenshot of the Attachments section

Open the quote and go to the Lines tab. Scroll to the Attachments section at the bottom of the page. Click Upload (or drag and drop a file into the upload area). The file is attached to the quote immediately.
Uploaded files are listed with their filename, size, and upload date. Click a file name to download or preview it.

Click the delete icon next to an attachment to remove it. Attachments on orders (converted quotes) are read-only and cannot be removed. note Attachments on a quote (type = Quote) can be managed freely. Once the quote becomes an Order, attachments are read-only.
TODO Confirm which file types are accepted and any size limits
When a quote is ready to submit as an order, use the Summary tab to review and check out.
Ensure the following are complete: All line items are configured correctly (Lines tab) Customer information and addresses are filled in (Customer tab) Any required attachments have been uploaded

Navigate to the Summary tab using the stage buttons at the top of the quote. The Summary provides a full read-only overview of the quote including all lines, pricing, and customer details. TODO Add screenshot of the Summary tab
From the Summary tab, click the Print button (printer icon) in the page header to generate a printable version of the quote.

TODO Document the checkout/payment flow — does it go through Stripe? Is there a PO number field? What happens after submission? After submission: The quote type changes from Quote to Order. An ERP order number is assigned (if integrated with an ERP system). You are redirected to the Thank You page confirming your order. The order is now visible on your Orders page.

If submitting as an order, you may be asked to provide a Customer PO number. This is displayed on the order and in any ERP integration. note Once a quote is submitted as an order, lines and attachments become read-only. Contact your administrator if changes are needed.
There are two ways to start a new quote in SWIFTSELL.
Navigate to Quotes in the main navigation. Click New Quote. SWIFTSELL creates a blank quote and opens it immediately. From the Lines tab, configure and add products.
documents.s: Option 1: Start from the Quote Dashboard

documents.t: Navigate to Product Catalog. Find the product you want and click Configure. SWIFTSELL creates a new quote automatically and opens the Configurator. Complete your configuration — when you click Finish, the product is added as Line 1 of your new quote.
documents.s: Option 2: Start from the Product Catalog

documents.t: If your administrator has enabled Quick Configure products, they appear as a product grid at the top of the Quote Dashboard. Click any product card to immediately open the Configurator with a new quote.

Once a quote exists, you can: Add more lines by using the Configure action from the Lines tab. Fill in customer and address details on the Customer tab. Attach supporting files on the Lines tab. Print or check out from the Summary tab.

A quote in SWIFTSELL is a collection of configured product line items with pricing, customer details, and supporting information. Quotes are used to present pricing to a customer and ultimately convert to an order.
The Quote Dashboard is your central hub for managing quotes. Access it from the main navigation bar. TODO Add screenshot of the Quote Dashboard

Quick Configure — if your administrator has set up products for fast configuration, they appear here as a product grid. Click any card to immediately open the Configurator and create a new quote. My Quotes — a table of all quotes you have created, sorted by most recently created. Click any row to open that quote. Quote Requests — if the Quote Requests feature is enabled, a separate table shows quotes submitted by unauthenticated visitors that are awaiting follow-up.

Type Description Quote A standard pricing document built by a logged-in user Order A quote that has been submitted and converted to an order Quote Request Created by a public (not logged-in) user via the configurator; requires a rep to follow up

Opening a quote shows a tabbed workflow: Stage Description Lines The line items on the quote — configured products, quantities, and prices Customer Billing and shipping address, and customer contact information Summary A printable overview of the full quote Navigate between stages using the stage buttons at the top of the quote, or the left/right arrow buttons. info The Summary stage is disabled until at least one line has been added to the quote.
Creating a Quote Managing Quote Lines Attachments Checkout
The Lines tab is the main working area of a quote. Each line represents one configured product. TODO Add screenshot of the Lines tab

Field Description Product Name and part number of the configured product Description Configuration description generated by the configurator Quantity Number of units Unit Price Price per unit as determined by the configurator Line Total Quantity × Unit Price Image Thumbnail of the configured product (if available)
Click the Configure or Add Line button on the Lines tab to open the Configurator and add a new product to the quote.
Click the edit icon on any line to re-open it in the Configurator with your previous selections loaded. Make your changes and click Finish to update the line.
Click the delete icon on a line to remove it from the quote. You will be asked to confirm before the line is deleted.
TODO Confirm whether quantity can be edited inline on the Lines tab or requires reconfiguring
The running total of all lines is displayed in the page header as you work.

Below the lines table, the Attachments section lets you upload supporting documents to the quote. See Attachments & Communications for details. note Lines cannot be added or edited once a quote has been converted to an order (type = Order).
